reasoning: Despite massive US and Israeli bombardments that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei around March 1, 2026, and targeted internal security and leadership headquarters, the Iranian regime remains in power as of March 15. Crucially, a March 12 Reuters report cited US intelligence indicating that Iran's leadership is 'still largely intact and is not at risk of collapse any time soon.' Furthermore, there are no reports of significant security force defections or urban areas falling outside government control. While the severe pressure makes the mid-to-long-term survival of the Islamic Republic uncertain, the short 16-day window remaining until the March 31, 2026 deadline makes a complete collapse or loss of >50% territory highly unlikely within this specific timeframe. I assign an 8% probability, acknowledging the extreme volatility of the situation and the non-zero chance of a sudden internal coup.
